signals.title: USD/CAD — Geopolitical Shock + US CPI
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3704 as WTI crude surges +3.58% to $101.6 following the Strait of Hormuz closure. Despite CAD's typical correlation with oil, the pair remains bid as the market pivots to US CPI risk and a 150bp Fed-BoC carry advantage. CAD COT LF net short -46,889 (26.4% long) reflects deep institutional bearishness. Geopolitical supply shocks usually support CAD, but the impending US inflation print and risk-off sentiment are currently overriding the energy tailwind. Watch for a break of 1.3750 if US CPI exceeds expectations.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.584 testing the upper bound of its recent range as the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry spread sustains the bid. JPY COT LF net short -61,340 (36.0% long) confirms crowded carry positioning. While VIX at 18.8 indicates normal volatility, the z-score of -1.91σ in the USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation suggests the pair is becoming less sensitive to Treasury yields and more sensitive to intervention headlines. Verbal warnings likely intensify if price holds above 158.00 ahead of the June BoJ meeting.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5950 faces a significant positioning mismatch. COT LF net short is -19,294 with long positioning at an extreme low of 18.5%, while the RBNZ maintains a hawkish bias for the May 27 meeting. This creates a high-conviction short-squeeze profile if the RBNZ signals a hike. The 150bp Fed-NZD carry advantage currently supports USD, but the extreme spec short positioning leaves the pair vulnerable to a rapid reversal on any hawkish RBNZ surprise. Watch 0.6000 for squeeze confirmation.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7226 shows a sharp divergence between institutional and retail flows. COT LF net long is +58,994 (78.3% long) while retail sentiment is 84% short. Retail is aggressively fading the institutional trend and the 60bp AUD carry premium provided by the RBA's hiking stance. This divergence typically precedes a continuation of the institutional trend as retail is forced to cover. Support holds at 0.7200; invalidation on a shift in RBA hiking rhetoric at the June 16 meeting.
